nodes.label: P11_T001
nodes.identity_summary: A budget-conscious student who prioritizes price and friend recommendations over brand loyalty, with strong preferences for gentle, fragrant products that last all day. Despite limited income, he's willing to experiment with new brands when the financial risk is low, and cares significantly about how others perceive his scent and grooming choices.

nodes.label: price-first-decision-maker
nodes.description: Consistently ranks price as the most important factor and makes purchase decisions primarily based on cost considerations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When buying any personal care product, rank these factors from most to least important: ['price','brand','reviews','ingredients','recommendation','fragrance','packaging','expert']
[SYNTHETIC] I'd pick Brand B at ₹199. Price matters a lot to me, and if the ingredients look better on the label, it's worth trying something new for ₹50 less.
[SYNTHETIC] Price compared to regular options (1), The Shark Tank credibility (2), The return policy safety net (3), The innovative technology (4). Price matters most to me.
[REAL] What is your approximate monthly household income range? Below_25k
nodes.predictive_value: When shown premium body wash concepts, this person would likely seek budget alternatives or wait for discounts because price is their primary decision filter.

nodes.label: friend-recommendation-driven
nodes.description: Places high trust in friends' product recommendations and considers them a top trigger for switching brands.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] What would make you switch away from your current body wash? Pick your top 3: ['friend_rec','influencer','ad_resonance']
[REAL] Is there a particular influencer or expert associated with these brands whose recommendations you find credible? Recommended by friends mostly
[SYNTHETIC] 1) Seeing it in your best friend's bathroom 2) Free sample sachets received with another order 3) A dermatologist's recommendation in a YouTube video
[SYNTHETIC] E - A close friend swears by it (4 points), A - A dermatologist recommends it (3 points)
nodes.predictive_value: When shown testimonials or social proof, this person would likely respond most strongly to friend-based recommendations rather than expert endorsements or celebrity testimonials.

nodes.label: fragrance-longevity-seeker
nodes.description: Prioritizes long-lasting fragrance and cares deeply about how he smells to others throughout the day.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How much do you care about how you smell to others throughout the day? Care a lot about smell. Grooming is a mix of both, slightly more about others' perception.
[REAL] How long should a body wash fragrance last on your skin? All_day
[REAL] Is there any small change or improvement you wish Pears or Man Matters could make? Smell should last longer.
[REAL] What fragrance families do you naturally gravitate toward? Woody_musky,fresh_citrus
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash ads emphasizing long-lasting fragrance or all-day scent protection, this person would likely be highly interested and willing to pay a moderate premium.

nodes.label: low-brand-loyalty-experimenter
nodes.description: Shows minimal brand loyalty and is open to switching products frequently based on price, recommendations, or availability.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How loyal are you to personal care brands? Low_loyalty
[SYNTHETIC] C) Different brands each time. I have low brand loyalty and try different options based on price and availability.
[REAL] If a brand you liked raised its price by 30%, what would you do? Look_alternatives
[SYNTHETIC] B) Don't subscribe - I like the freedom to switch brands anytime. My loyalty is low and I enjoy trying new products when I discover them.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new brand launches or competitive alternatives, this person would likely be willing to switch from current brands without strong emotional attachment.

nodes.label: gentle-natural-ingredient-preferrer
nodes.description: Strongly prefers natural, chemical-free products and values gentleness on skin over advanced formulations.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] How important is it to you that a product is 'natural' or 'chemical-free'? {'rating':4,'explanation':'No side effects, only natural products'}
[REAL] What do you like MOST about your current body wash? Pears - smell, gentle
[SYNTHETIC] A - The simple ingredients body wash. I prefer natural, chemical-free products with no side effects. Coconut oil, aloe vera, and honey sound gentler and more trustworthy.
[REAL] Which certifications or labels influence your purchase? Ayurvedic,ph_balanced,paraben_free,sulfate_free,dermatologist_tested
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts with natural ingredients versus clinical formulations, this person would likely choose the natural option even if it costs slightly more.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-dependent
nodes.description: Primarily relies on quick commerce apps like Blinkit/Zepto for body wash purchases and adapts choices based on limited selection.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Where do you most often buy personal care products? ['quick_commerce']
[REAL] Do you use quick commerce for personal care? Yes, limited selection changes purchases. Try new brands on quick commerce; selection based on price and brand name recall.
[SYNTHETIC] Order a familiar brand without further research. On Blinkit I usually go with Pears or check price and brand name recall for quick decisions.
[SYNTHETIC] Pick from Zepto's limited range for instant delivery. Limited selection changes my purchases anyway, and I can find something acceptable from 4 options.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown distribution strategies, this person would likely respond best to brands that prioritize quick commerce availability over traditional retail presence.

nodes.label: reviews-before-unknown-brands
nodes.description: Consistently seeks online reviews and ratings before trying unfamiliar brands, especially on Amazon/Nykaa.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] When shopping online for personal care, what do you look at first? ['price','brand_name','star_rating']
[SYNTHETIC] Search for the product on Amazon/Nykaa to read reviews before ordering on Zepto. I need reviews before trying new products.
[SYNTHETIC] Wait for it to appear on Amazon/Nykaa with 100+ reviews. I need reviews and ratings before trying new brands.
[SYNTHETIC] D - Quickly check Amazon reviews on my phone before choosing. I always check reviews before buying unknown brands
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new product launches, this person would likely wait for substantial review accumulation before purchasing rather than being an early adopter.

nodes.label: indian-brand-preference

[REAL] Would you be interested in a body wash specifically formulated for Indian skin types and hard water conditions? {'rating':4,'explanation':'Specific to Indian skin tones and textures.,'}
[SYNTHETIC] Made in India at ₹249. I prefer Indian brands as they're tailored to Indian skin, plus I'm willing to pay 20-40% premium for Made in India, not 80% less.
[REAL] What is the maximum premium (%) you would pay for a product that aligns with your values? 'Made in India':'20_40'
nodes.predictive_value: When shown Indian versus international brand options at similar price points, this person would likely choose the Indian brand and use origin as a key differentiator.

nodes.label: discount-opportunity-seizer
nodes.description: Actively seeks and responds strongly to discounts, deals, and money-saving opportunities even when not immediately needed.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] B) Buy the combo to save money even though you don't need the second one yet. Saving ₹149 is significant for my budget, and I'll use the second one eventually.
[SYNTHETIC] A) Use the coupon now — great deal, I'll stock up. ₹249 for ₹399 product is too good to miss.
[SYNTHETIC] Wait 5 days for the Amazon delivery at ₹199. ₹100 savings is too much to ignore - I can manage with soap for a few days
[SYNTHETIC] Drive to DMart to save ₹50. Every rupee matters on my student budget.
nodes.predictive_value: When shown limited-time offers or bulk purchase deals, this person would likely make immediate purchase decisions to capture savings even if it means buying more than currently needed.

[SYNTHETIC] B) Feel slightly conscious and consider upgrading. I care about others' perception and notice what people use.

nodes.label: sample-first-risk-reducer

[SYNTHETIC] The discovery kit for ₹299. Good value to try different formulations without committing to a full bottle.

nodes.label: texture-and-lather-prioritizer

nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts from trusted brands, this person would likely view them favorably but prioritize functional benefits and price over brand reputation alone.

nodes.label: convenience-valued-but-not-primary-driver
nodes.description: Rates convenience as moderately important (4/5) and shows interest in time-saving aspects but price and quality remain primary concerns.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Convenience / ease of use: 4
[SURVEY] How well does the Dove 60-Second Body Spray concept fit with your current routine? A: 4
nodes.predictive_value: When shown convenient body wash formats, this person would likely appreciate the convenience but still evaluate primarily based on price and quality benefits.

nodes.label: cleansing-effectiveness-expected-baseline
nodes.description: Rates cleansing effectiveness as important (4/5) but focuses more on additional benefits like fragrance and moisturization in feedback.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Cleansing effectiveness: 4
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts, this person would likely assume cleansing effectiveness as a given and focus evaluation on fragrance, moisturization, and price.

nodes.label: evening-routine-relaxation-appealing
nodes.description: Shows specific interest in calming evening ritual concept and relaxation benefits for nighttime use.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] The calming ritual idea sounds nice for evening relaxation
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts positioned for evening or relaxation use, this person would likely find the ritual aspect appealing and consider it a positive differentiator.

nodes.label: multi-brand-user-open-to-switching
nodes.description: Uses multiple brands (Pears, Dove, Nivea) in past 3 months, showing willingness to try different options rather than brand loyalty.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] Which brands have you used in the past 3 months? A: ['Pears', 'Dove', 'Nivea']
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new body wash brands or concepts, this person would likely be open to trying them if they meet their price, fragrance, and quality criteria.

nodes.label: customization-concept-intriguing-but-price-sensitive
nodes.description: Shows interest in personalized and customization concepts but consistently mentions price concerns for these premium offerings.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] The personalized fragrance concept is interesting, but at ₹799-999 it's way too expensive
[SURVEY] The AI skin analysis technology is interesting and the customization concept could be useful
[SURVEY] The initial price at ₹699 is too high for my budget
nodes.predictive_value: When shown customized body wash concepts, this person would likely express interest in the personalization but require significantly lower pricing to consider purchasing.

nodes.label: daily-usage-routine-established
nodes.description: Uses body wash daily, indicating established routine and regular consumption pattern.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] How often do you personally use body wash or shower gel? A: Daily
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts, this person would likely evaluate them based on how well they fit into a daily routine and consider cost-per-use over time.

nodes.label: skip-concept-most-aligned-with-needs
nodes.description: Ranks Dove Skip as most appealing concept and rates it highest for personal relevance and brand fit.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] please rank them from most to least appealing A: ['Dove Skip', 'Dove Night Wash', 'Dove 60-Second Body Spray']
[SURVEY] How relevant is the Dove Skip to your personal needs? A: Very relevant
[SURVEY] How well does the Dove Skip idea fit with what you expect from the Dove brand? A: Fits very well
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts that combine moisturizing benefits with time-saving and money-saving aspects, this person would likely show highest interest and purchase intent.

nodes.label: price-reduction-suggestions-specific
nodes.description: Provides specific price points they would find acceptable, showing clear mental pricing framework for different product types.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] if they made individual bottles at ₹299-399 with good fragrance options, it could work
[SURVEY] What would you expect to pay for your most preferred concept? A: 299
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts above their price threshold, this person would likely suggest specific lower price points that would make the product acceptable to them.

nodes.label: believability-concerns-for-bold-claims
nodes.description: Consistently rates product claims as only 'somewhat believable' across all concepts, showing measured skepticism.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] How believable are the claims made about the Dove 60-Second Body Spray? A: Somewhat believable
[SURVEY] How believable are the claims made about the Dove Skip? A: Somewhat believable
[SURVEY] How believable are the claims made about the Dove Night Wash? A: Somewhat believable
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts with strong benefit claims, this person would likely maintain skepticism and require additional proof or evidence before fully believing the claims.

links.mechanism: Strong price sensitivity makes him highly responsive to deals and savings opportunities, even when not immediately needed.
Lack of strong brand attachment makes him more open to switching based on trusted friend recommendations.
Preference for natural, chemical-free products aligns with and reinforces trust in traditional Ayurvedic formulations.
Need for review validation drives preference for established platforms like Amazon/Nykaa that provide reliable review systems.
Both traits reflect cultural values and respect for traditional Indian approaches to personal care.
Caring about how others perceive his smell drives the need for long-lasting fragrance that performs throughout the day.
Prioritizing convenience naturally leads to reliance on quick commerce platforms that offer immediate delivery.
Skepticism toward marketing claims drives preference for direct experience and user testimonials over theoretical benefits.
Limited income (below ₹25k) directly drives the need to prioritize price above all other factors in purchase decisions.
Limited budget makes the financial risk of trying new full-size products too high, driving preference for low-cost trial options.
Limited budget creates tension with willingness to pay premiums, resulting in very selective premium spending only for high-value benefits.
Both traits stem from caring about how others perceive him, making scent an essential rather than optional feature.
Creates internal tension between habitual repurchasing when satisfied versus stated openness to switching brands frequently.
Since fragrance is critical but cannot be fully evaluated without testing, the need for trial sizes becomes essential to assess this key decision factor.
Budget constraints drive the need to test products at lower price points before committing to full-size purchases that exceed their price threshold.
High priority on moisturization directly drives preference for the Skip concept which offers moisturizing benefits that eliminate need for separate lotion.
Interest in time-saving aligns with Skip concept's promise to eliminate the extra step of applying lotion after showering.
While they find innovative concepts interesting, their budget constraints prevent them from paying premium prices typically associated with innovative products.
Skepticism about whether products work as claimed reinforces the need to test products before making full-size purchases.
Moderate satisfaction with current products creates openness to trying different brands that might better meet their needs.
Living situation where rushing between classes creates specific need for quick, efficient personal care routines.
Strong focus on value drives specific price expectations and creates resistance to products that don't meet their cost-benefit analysis.
Shows pattern of finding innovative concepts intellectually interesting but not being willing to pay premium prices for innovation alone.
